scope.research_question: Within synthetic student response research, how are human labels, AI scores, model-generated labels, rubric scores, or scorer agreement used to evaluate, label, validate, or benchmark generated responses, and what belongs in the first public baseline for this review question?

summary: Created a staged first literature-review baseline for synthetic response scoring agreement, including a targeted search protocol, six scoring/labeling extracts, six findings, one bounded synthesis claim, and source-access metadata for direct-support sources.
next_actions: After publication, check the public review page and synthesis matrix for the narrowed wording.
Keep any later human-AI scoring-agreement work constrained to sources with verified synthetic student response corpora.
Use abstract-only screening decisions for discovery, not direct support, unless full text is obtained.
